premise: When my mother’s Alzheimer’s worsened, my sister convinced the family to make her sole power of attorney by painting me as grief-stricken and incompetent. She then secretly deeded Mom’s $280,000 condo to herself, dumped our mother in a bare-bones Medicaid nursing home two states away, and told everyone I had stolen the estate. For three years I worked under a false identity as a companion in that very facility, gathering a paper trail of her Medicaid fraud and undue influence, then sued for elder exploitation. The court voided the deed, awarded me the condo, and hit her with a $150,000 reimbursement judgment — now Mom lives in a top-tier memory care unit I fund with my sister’s seized assets, and my sister is permanently banned from visiting after she tried to sneak in disguised as a volunteer.
